retry_reason: A put always succeeds in decrementing the reference count; once the refcount reaches zero, the object is freed. The caller must not attempt to retry a put on an already-released object.
contract_summary: This function releases one reference on a btrfs_transaction. It decrements the use_count and, if the count reaches zero, performs final cleanup (validates lists, processes deleted block groups, and kfrees the transaction). After calling btrfs_put_transaction, the caller must not access the transaction pointer as it may have been freed. This is the release side of the reference-count lifecycle; it must be paired with a prior get (e.g., by btrfs_get_transaction or similar). The caller does not need to check a return value; the put is unconditional and irreversible.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement the transaction's use_count reference and free the transaction when the last reference is released. The cleanup code (BUG_ON, WARN_ON, list processing) is inherent to the final destruction phase and not separate business logic.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test is called on &transaction->use_count, which decrements and tests the refcount. The access path transaction.use_count.refs and type chain btrfs_transaction--refcount_t--atomic_t are consistent with the code and callee info.
